contract.goal: Ship the operator-invoked feedback loop. Gather a PR's review comments, classify each (fixed-intent / ambiguous / stale / discussion-only) with confidence, surface the substrate's pass, grill ONLY ambiguous + low-confidence classifications, dispatch implementer agents for high-confidence fixed-intent comments, and resolve handled review threads so re-invocation is safe. Yield-to-operator: the substrate NEVER auto-resolves an ambiguous comment.
acceptance_criteria: cli/lib/feedback.ts classifyComment(comment) -> {classification, confidence, derivation}: pure, signal-counting; stale from GitHub's outdated flag; question/hedge checked BEFORE imperative (so 'should we rename X?' is ambiguous); reuses CONFIDENCE/GRILL_THRESHOLD from pr.ts.

cli/lib/feedback.ts buildDispatchTasks(classified) -> Task[]: THE yield invariant — ONLY high-confidence fixed-intent -> tasks; ambiguous/stale/discussion-only/low-confidence -> ZERO tasks. Enforced in pure code, not skill prose.
cli/lib/feedback.ts filterUnhandled(comments, resolvedIds) -> comments: pure dedupe on resolved thread id (comment-id fallback) for idempotent re-runs.
cli/jelly-run.ts wires verbs classify-comments + build-dispatch-tasks (5 verbs total); registry tripwire updated.

cli/lib/feedback.test.ts leads with the yield invariant (asserts the NEGATIVE per class: ambiguous/stale/discussion/low-confidence -> empty task list) + the question-beats-imperative asymmetry + filterUnhandled dedupe.

skills/jelly-pr-feedback/SKILL.md: THIN glue, interactive-only, explicit turn-based protocol (operator -> substrate -> agent -> loop); resolveReviewThread idempotency; never promotes an ambiguous comment without the operator.
rules_applied: Whiteboard: the yield invariant is the single most important test — buildDispatchTasks dispatches only high-confidence fixed-intent; everything else yields. Enforced in pure code (lib/feedback.ts), tested with negative assertions.

Whiteboard idempotency dedupe-key (operator decision F-dedupe): resolve the review thread (GraphQL resolveReviewThread); classify reads resolved-state via filterUnhandled and skips. GitHub-native durable state, no local ledger.

Yield-to-operator (non-negotiable): /jelly-pr-feedback is interactive-only (no --mode=auto); the substrate classifies + surfaces, then yields; only the operator's answer can promote an ambiguous comment to a task.
Confidence model reused from pr.ts (CONFIDENCE/GRILL_THRESHOLD) — single-sourced threshold/band vocabulary across the plugin.
Lossy-subagent-boundary (RESEARCH § Phase 1.1): implementer dispatch results land via a side-channel, not the Agent return value.
Node strip-only honored (all 7 new/modified modules load under real node). Panel: contract-fit + test-unit + naming (dropped react-api) — all approved first run.
Scope-shift step 5: single signal (phase boundary — last unit in Phase 2.1); no second concurring signal, so no plan-revision offered (noted below).
disqualifiers: Auto-resolving an ambiguous comment without the operator.
A --mode=auto path that bypasses the operator on ambiguity.
Classification logic in skill prose instead of the CLI verb.
A local handled-comments ledger (use GitHub resolved-thread state).

actions: cli/lib/feedback.ts: CommentClassification union + ReviewComment/ClassifiedComment/DispatchTask types. classifyComment with precedence outdated->stale (high), then QUESTION_OR_HEDGE_RE->ambiguous (high — a question trumps any imperative verb, the yield bias), then IMPERATIVE_RE->fixed-intent (high if short/directive, medium if buried in a long comment so it grills), then DISCUSSION_RE->discussion-only (high), else ambiguous (low, always grills — never guesses fixed-intent). buildDispatchTasks filters to classification==='fixed-intent' && confidence>=GRILL_THRESHOLD. filterUnhandled drops comments whose threadId (or id) is in the resolved set.

cli/verbs/classify-comments.ts: reads ReviewComment[] from --file or stdin (fd 0), --resolved=<id,...> for dedupe, filterUnhandled + classify, emits ClassifiedComment[] JSON. cli/verbs/build-dispatch-tasks.ts: reads ClassifiedComment[] from --file/stdin, emits DispatchTask[] JSON.
Wired both verbs into cli/jelly-run.ts (NAMESPACES + VERBLESS_NAMESPACES + VERBS_BY_NAMESPACE; now 5 verbless verbs). Updated jelly-run.test.ts registry tripwire to the 5-verb set.

cli/lib/feedback.test.ts (12 tests): leads with the yield invariant (mixed set -> exactly the one high-confidence fixed-intent survives; per-class negatives -> empty task list; low/medium fixed-intent -> empty); question-beats-imperative ('should we rename X?' -> ambiguous, not dispatched); outdated-directive -> stale (not dispatched); praise -> discussion-only; unclassifiable -> ambiguous@low; filterUnhandled dedupe (thread + id fallback). Threshold imported from pr.ts (calibration-drift guard).

skills/jelly-pr-feedback/SKILL.md: thin-glue, interactive-only body with the explicit turn-based protocol — gather comments (gh) -> classify-comments (--resolved skips resolved threads) -> surface the substrate pass -> grill ONLY ambiguous + low-confidence (operator promotes or leaves) -> build-dispatch-tasks -> dispatch implementers (Agent, results to side-channel) -> resolveReviewThread for handled threads -> report + loop back.

Verified: npx vitest run -> 80 files, 1188 passed (was 1174; +14), 0 failures. Strip-only real-node load of feedback.ts + both verbs + entry: all load. Live e2e (echo comments | classify-comments | build-dispatch-tasks): rename-directive -> fixed-intent@0.9 -> the ONLY dispatched task; 'should we extract this?' -> ambiguous@0.9 (not dispatched, question beats the imperative); 'lgtm nice' -> discussion-only (not dispatched); outdated directive -> stale (not dispatched). Yield invariant holds end-to-end. Panel (contract-fit + test-unit + naming) all approved first run.

notes_for_pr: signal: phase-boundary: U3 is the last unit in Phase 2.1 (single signal; no revise offered).

Phase 2.1 complete: jelly-run plugin ships the scaffold (U1), /jelly-run + /jelly-pr + testable CLI core (U2), and /jelly-pr-feedback (U3). The three skills are exercised end-to-end only in the M3 dogfood (Phase 3.1); the CLI core is fully unit-tested (67 jelly-run tests). The F1 /goal-yield assumption (sequential skill composition) remains flagged for M3 verification.
